title: Finnish candle
description: Also known as a taiga candle. Convenient for cooking, but noticeably worse than other lays at warming or lighting the area around it. The simple version needs a log at least 20 cm in diameter and some wire: split the log into 4-8 wedges, plane the inner faces, reassemble it, and cinch it back together with wire, leaving small gaps for airflow. The classic version cuts 70-80% of the way through the log instead of splitting it fully. Depending on size, a candle like this burns anywhere from half an hour to four hours.
steps: Take a dry log at least 20 cm in diameter.
Split it into 4-8 wedges (or saw down 70-80% of the log's length without splitting it all the way through).
Plane or shave the inner faces of the wedges to make room for tinder.
Reassemble the log around tinder placed in the center, and cinch it together with wire around the outside, leaving small gaps for airflow.
Light the tinder from the top center. For easier cooking, dig the candle in slightly or use a flat-cut top so a pot sits securely on top.
